description_long: This house features a traditional two-story structure with a prominent front porch supported by columns, evoking a classic American residential style. The front facade is symmetrical, with evenly spaced windows and a central entryway, while the rear and side sections are dramatically juxtaposed with angular, modern extensions that appear to be constructed from concrete or stone. The roofline is complex, combining a gable roof over the main house with irregular, jagged forms in the rear, possibly representing a contemporary architectural intervention. The exterior is primarily white, with the modern addition displaying a weathered, stone-like texture. The house sits on a rectangular plot of green lawn, bordered by paved walkways and a driveway. The overall appearance blends traditional and avant-garde elements, making it visually striking and architecturally unique.
description: A two-story white house with a classic front porch and symmetrical windows, dramatically contrasted by a modern, angular stone extension at the rear. The structure sits on a grassy plot with paved paths and a driveway.
description_view_0: Front view shows the symmetrical facade, central entryway with porch, and traditional windows, with the modern stone extension visible behind.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the transition from the classic house to the angular, weathered stone addition, and shows the driveway curving alongside.
description_view_2: Rear view emphasizes the jagged, modern stone structure dominating the back, with minimal traditional features visible.
description_view_3: Right side view displays both the traditional house and the modern extension, with visible steps and a side entry, as well as the lawn and walkways.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on main facade, irregular on extension
attribute_entryway_design: Central front door with steps, covered porch supported by columns, traditional detailing
